meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in refuge, mourning, and town memory

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Old town buildings, courthouse memory, and northern California civic texture.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional family, road, and mining-community context.

image_prompt.subject: a small 1899 churchyard with a weathered bell frame, grave fence, muddy path, and riders waiting respectfully outside
image_prompt.scene: cool gray morning; pine hills, plain wooden grave boards with no readable names, a wagon with a covered load, borrowed blankets near the door, and a bell rope moving slightly in wind
image_prompt.composition: square solemn exterior with the fence line separating road and yard; shelter and accusation held in the same quiet frame
image_prompt.camera: eye-level from just outside the gate, restrained and respectful, with no theatrical beam of light
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Let stillness do the work; the yard should hold shelter and accusation at once.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine town-edge scene
render_traits: slightly simplified riders, fence, wagon, and church silhouettes
modest texture resolution on paint, rope, mud, wool, and grass
hard ambient occlusion under fence rails, wagon wheels, grave boards, and doorway
believable 1899 churchyard, road, and clothing materials
restrained mourning composition rather than sentimental postcard
style_modifiers.palette: wet grass green, weathered white paint, mud brown, wool black, gray sky, pine green, rope tan, and wagon canvas beige.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable grave names, signs, notices, labels, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
cropped bell frame or cropped grave fence

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The churchyard, bell frame, grave fence, wagon, riders, and muddy path are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 town-edge life.
The image is restrained and grounded rather than sentimental or theatrical.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
